content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
-l'.!8 J S1rcc1 • Suite 620 • SiJcramcnto. CA 95814-2329 
(9 I 6) .l::!2-5660 • r:ilx (91 flJ 3'.!::? 0886 
Ramon Medina 
4941 E. 60th Street 
Maywood, CA 90270 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No, W-12-108 
Dear Mr. Medina: 
July 18, 2012 
We received your request for advice concerning the Maywood Mayor. Under the 
provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329, unless you are an authorized representative 
of the mayor, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide 
analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(8) and (c)(I).) It does not appear you are authorized. 
Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of Regulation 18329 for your review. 
Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the 
Commission at (916) 322-5660. If you wish to file a complaint, a complaint form can be 
obtained from the FPPC Website at http://www.fppc.ca.gov/index.php?id=498. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HMR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Heather M. Rowan 
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations 18329, unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the mayor, the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis. It does not appear the requestor is authorized. Therefore, the request is withdrawn.

Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of Regulation 18329 for your review.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice to Ramon Medina regarding questions about the Maywood Mayor?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requestor is not an authorized representative of the Maywood Mayor as required by Regulation 18329. The request was withdrawn.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Withdrawal letter with clear conclusion but no formal QUESTION section. Request was denied on jurisdictional/standing grounds rather than on merits.

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations 18329, unless the requestor is an authorized representative of the mayor, the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis. It does not appear the requestor is authorized. Therefore, the request is withdrawn.
